title.en: Leap Years in the Jewish Calendar: Adding a Whole Month
title.zh: 犹太历中的闰年：增加整整一个月
title.he: שנות המעוברות בלוח העברי: הוספת חודש שלם

author: Torah Light Editorial Team

summary.en: Explores the Jewish calendar's leap year system, explaining why Adar II is added seven times every 19 years to align lunar months with solar seasons, maintaining the proper timing of holidays and agricultural connections.
summary.zh: 探讨犹太历闰年系统，解释为什么每19年增加七次第二个阿达尔月，以使阴历月份与阳历季节保持一致，维持节日的适当时机和农业联系。
summary.he: חוקר את מערכת השנים המעוברות של הלוח העברי, מסביר מדוע אדר ב' מתווסף שבע פעמים כל 19 שנה כדי ליישר חודשים ירחיים עם עונות שמשיות, תוך שמירה על תזמון נכון של חגים וקשרים חקלאיים.

key_points: Jewish calendar operates on lunar system but must align with solar seasons
Adar II added 7 times every 19 years following Metonic cycle
Prevents religious holidays from drifting through seasons
System formalized by Hillel II around 358 CE
Demonstrates remarkable astronomical accuracy for ancient system

chinese_dynasties: Han Dynasty (202 BCE) - mentioned as contemporary to early Jewish calendar adjustments
Tang Dynasty - referenced for parallel astronomical developments
Jin Dynasty (358 CE) - contemporary to Hillel II's calendar formalization

image_description: A circular diagram showing the 19-year Metonic cycle with Hebrew months arranged in a spiral, highlighting the seven leap years when Adar II (marked in gold) is added. Ancient scrolls and astronomical instruments from the Second Temple period frame the image.
